Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Anomaly Detection Investigation Plan

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case ID 1002 Anomaly Description Confirm Shipment event id 9 occurs before Register Order event id 8 in the process flow

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Potential Root Cause s System error Event logging timestamp mismatch Manual override Logistics manager manually confirmed shipment before order was officially registered due to urgent demand Data entry error Incorrect timestamp or event sequence in log

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case ID 1004 Anomaly Description Receive Payment event id 22 occurs before Register Order event id 21 and Issue Invoice event id 23

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Potential Root Cause s System glitch Payment received before order was created pre payment or duplicate record Fraudulent activity Customer made payment without placing order Data inconsistency Event log timestamp out of order due to system delay or clock sync issue

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Case ID 1003 Ship Goods event id 17 occurs before Validate Stock event id 3 in case 1001 but in case 1003 Ship Goods at event id 17 occurs after Perform Credit Check but before Validate Stock Wait this is actually not an anomaly because Validate Stock is missing entirely in case 1003

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Process deviation Stock validation skipped due to urgency or system failure Manual override Shipment processed without stock check backorder or expedited handling

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case ID 1004 Confirm Shipment event id 24 has shipment scheduled N yet Ship Goods event id 25 occurs afterward

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Potential Root Cause s Logical inconsistency Shipment cannot be confirmed as scheduled if not scheduled Data entry error shipment scheduled field incorrectly set to N Process bypass Shipment initiated without formal confirmation emergency dispatch

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Case 1002 The Confirm Shipment event occurs before Register Order which violates the assumed process flow This suggests either a logging error where timestamps are misaligned a business exception where logistics initiated shipment prematurely due to urgency priority order or data corruption in the event log incorrect event id assignment or misordered entries

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Case 1004 Receive Payment occurs before Register Order which is highly unusual Possible explanations Pre payment Customer paid before placing the order deposit or full payment upfront System integration issue Payment system and order system are out of sync Fraud or duplicate record Payment recorded for a non existent or duplicate order Timestamp error Payment timestamp is inaccurate due to server time misconfiguration

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Case 1003 Missing Validate Stock event This could indicate Process bypass Stock check skipped due to high demand or system outage Automated process failure Stock validation step not triggered or failed silently Manual override Warehouse staff shipped without verification

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Case 1004 Confirm Shipment has shipment scheduled N but Ship Goods follows This suggests Inconsistent data The shipment scheduling status was not updated correctly Process deviation Shipment initiated without formal scheduling emergency or expedited handling System bug Confirmation step failed to update the shipment scheduled flag

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Investigate Event Order and Timestamps in Case 1002 SELECT case id event id activity timestamp resource additional info FROM order event log WHERE case id equals 1002 ORDER BY timestamp Purpose Verify if the actual sequence of events matches the event id order or if timestamps are misaligned

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check for Pre Payment or Out of Order Events in Case 1004 SELECT case id activity timestamp role department additional info FROM order event log join resources WHERE case id equals 1004 ORDER BY timestamp Purpose Examine the full sequence of events and roles involved to detect anomalies in process flow

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Validate Stock Check Presence and Timing Across Cases SELECT case id stock check time shipment time FROM order event log grouped by case id having shipment time less than stock check time or stock check time is null Purpose Identify cases where shipment occurs before stock validation or where stock validation is missing

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check Shipment Scheduling Status versus Shipment Execution SELECT case id activity timestamp additional info FROM order event log WHERE activity equals Confirm Shipment and additional info like shipment scheduled N Purpose Find all instances where shipment confirmation indicates not scheduled yet shipment occurs afterward

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Cross Reference Orders and Event Logs for Payment Order Mismatches SELECT case id activity timestamp order value order type additional info FROM order event log join orders WHERE activity equals Receive Payment and case id not in select case id from order event log where activity equals Register Order Purpose Identify payment events occurring without a corresponding order registration

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Multiple anomalies exist in the event log particularly around process sequence violations missing critical steps and potential data inconsistencies

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: These could stem from system errors manual overrides fraud or process deviations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The proposed SQL queries will help validate these hypotheses by analyzing temporal order resource involvement and event content Further analysis should focus on timestamp accuracy role based process adherence and cross table consistency between order event log orders and resources
